title: Health/Disability Related Support Groups * Blindness - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Health/Disability Related Support Groups * Blindness 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Amputations Autism Spectrum Disorder Blindness Deafness Hard of Hearing Parkinson's Disease AIDS/HIV Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AMERICAN COUNCIL OF THE BLIND AMERICAN COUNCIL OF THE BLIND OREGON SUPPORT GROUPS FOR BLIND OR VISUALLY IMPAIRED INDIVIDUALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support groups for people who are blind or visually impaired, as well as people living with and/or caring for people who are blind or visually impaired. Groups occur during monthly meetings, which includes the group's business meeting and entertainment. Transportation can be provided to most meetings and events, if arranged in advance. Provides a monthly newsletter that informs members of upcoming activities and items of interest to the blind community. Confidential Address Eligibility: People who are blind or visually impaired, as well as people living with and/or caring for people who are blind or visually impaired or those interested in working with the blind or visually impaired Hours: Varies by service (541) 269-1993 Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Also provides independent living services for visually impaired individuals. Includes orientation and mobility training, techniques of daily living, Braille, counseling, support groups, vocational training, job development, and transition services for blind high school students. Also offers a selection of adaptive items for people with visual impairments. 541 Willamette Avenue #408 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service; adults certified legally blind or diagnosed with condition leading to blindness, or age 55 years and above and unable to drive or read newspaper with glasses. Age 55 and older for independent living services. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-7990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults.
